description: Misericordia University in Dallas, Pa., offers an online accounting degree . Distance learners can earn Misericordia’s bachelor’s of science in accounting in just two-and-a-half years of part-time study through the school’s accelerated degree program. The accelerated program is designed for working adults with prior college credits or relevant work experience. The program is structured into into seven-and-a-half-week terms offered eight times per year, and students with previous accounting experience are eligible to apply for prior learning credit. Distance learners take courses, including principles of marketing, macroeconomics, and a seminar in business policies. Students gain real-world experience by completing a mandatory six-credit internship at an accounting firm or company near home. Incoming freshmen need a minimum 2.5 GPA and a minimum 850 SAT score (combined math and critical reading). Transfer students must hold at least 15 college credits with a minimum 2.0 GPA.

Distance learners can earn an online accounting degree at Keiser University-Fort Lauderdale in Florida. Keiser offers more than 100 career and academic degrees from the associate through the doctoral level and serves more than 16,000 students annually. Graduates of Keiser's BA in accounting program are prepared to work as entry-level accountants in business, government, and the nonprofit sector. Keiser not only provides students with a broad overview of the field, it also instills an advanced understanding of specialties within accounting. Topics covered include ethics, communication skills, taxation, and auditing. Students must complete the following three-credit prerequisites before enrolling in any accounting courses: microeconomics, macroeconomics, and statistics. To graduate with the 120-credit bachelor’s degree, students must complete 45 general education credits and 75 major credits. Auditing courses available to online accounting students include income tax accounting, advanced managerial accounting, and project management.

Students interested in getting their accounting degree online find an excellent option at Touro University Worldwide, based in Los Alamitos, CA. The self-paced program is fully online, with no in-person requirements. Each course lasts eight weeks and each tuition credit costs a flat rate, regardless of where you live. The bachelor’s of science in business administration (BSBA) with an accounting concentration at Touro requires 120 credits for completion. This includes 45 general education credits, 39 core credit requirements, 24 elective credits, and 12 credits in a concentration. Accounting is one of the seven available concentrations for BSBA students. Individuals who select the accounting concentration can work in financial accounting, financial reporting analysis, and cash management. Students enrolled in the BSBA with an accounting concentration program can graduate after three-and-a-half years of full-time study. Transfer students who bring in credits from previous study complete the program more quickly.

Indiana Wesleyan University-Marion offers an accounting degree online through its BS in accounting program. Distance learners can complete the entire program online, with no required campus visits. The degree is designed for adults already working in the accounting field and focuses on the core issues of accounting theory, auditing, and taxation. Graduates have the skills needed to enter into or advance in accounting. The degree also provides a solid foundation for individuals interested in taking the CPA exam to be become certified. The degree can be completed in just 18 to 20 months if previous college credits transfer. Elearners benefit from a supportive cohort structure, in which they go through the program and take classes with the same group of students. Tuition is the same cost regardless of where individuals live.

Students searching for online accounting programs will be interested in the bachelor’s in business administration (BBA) with a concentration in accounting from the Fox School of Business at Temple University in Philadelphia. The accelerated bachelor’s program takes just three years to complete, and students can earn their master’s of accountancy (MAcc) with one more additional year of study. The program covers accounting fundamentals necessary to succeed in entry-level positions, including taxation, ethics, and International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS). The BBA is a good starting point for individuals interested in becoming CPAs. Faculty advisors help students figure out how the program can help them complete the requirements and prepare for taking the CPA exam. The same faculty members who teach on the physical Temple campus also teach the online courses, ensuring students get the same high-quality instruction. Numerous local and regional accounting firms recruit accounting students from Temple’s BBA program.

Champlain College in Burlington, Vt., offers distance learners the opportunity to earn an accredited online accounting degree . Students must complete 120 credits in order to graduate from the program -- 57 in general education and 63 in accounting. The bachelor’s of science in accounting degree is designed for busy working adults, with most courses presented in accelerated seven-week sessions. Classes offered include managing behavior in organizations, cost accounting, and decision making through critical thinking. The program strives to provide students with a solid understanding of the broad theories of accounting, as well as practical skills needed to succeed in the field today. Online learners may also earn certificates in accounting, advanced accounting, or cost accounting as they progress through the curriculum. As students approach graduation, they take an accounting capstone course, in which they reflect on their time in the program, identify current problems or questions in the field of accounting, and research solutions.

Students who want to earn a degree at one of the accounting programs online will be interested to learn the details of the bachelor’s of science in business-accounting online program at the American Women’s College at Bay Path University in Longmeadow, Mass. Bay Path is a private university with a history of supporting female students. The accounting program provides instruction in the fundamentals of accounting theory, like auditing, business ethics/laws, and financial accounting. Available courses include federal income tax concepts, financial reporting, and contemporary issues in accounting. Bay Path also requires students to take courses related to career development, like strategies for career growth and leadership in practice. Courses last six weeks, and there are six sessions each year. The accelerated format makes it possible to earn the BS in one to three-and-a-half years. Up to 90 credits can be transferred into the program.

An accredited online accounting degree is available for distance learners at Saint Joseph’s College of Maine in the town of Standish. The BS in interdisciplinary science with an accounting minor consists of 128 credits. Transfer students appreciate that only 25% of the required credits must be completed at Saint Joseph’s, allowing them to transfer up to 75% of the credits needed to graduate. The degree is available entirely online, with no on-campus requirements. Additionally, students interested in the master’s of accountancy degree at Saint Joseph’s have the option to fast-track the application process, as coursework for the bachelor’s includes most prerequisites needed for the master’s program. Program faculty have real-world, practical experience, and the curriculum helps prepare students to sit for the CPA exam. Students take six accounting courses (18 credits), choosing from classes like business finance, federal income taxes, and managerial accounting.

Viterbo University in La Crosse, Wis., offers an online accounting degree designed for individuals who already hold an accounting associate degree or have completed certain prerequisite accounting courses. Viterbo is a private Roman Catholic and Franciscan institution, and all students must take two religious courses. The bachelor’s of business administration in accounting program at Viterbo can be earned 100% online, with no on-campus requirements. The program has a generous transfer credit policy, allowing transfer students to earn their bachelor’s more quickly. Students can also apply to get academic credit for prior learning received through on-the-job training and experience. Graduates of the program are prepared to take the CPA exam upon completing 150 credit hours. Online students benefit from special online tutoring, eight-week terms, and personal advising services. Courses offered include external reporting to strategic management, applied business statistics to legal aspects of financial and commercial transactions.

An accredited online accounting degree is available at Hiram College in Hiram, Ohio. Distance learners can earn a bachelor’s of accounting and financial management 100% online, or they can choose to enroll in a blended program that combines on-campus and elearning. The program prepares graduates to work in entry-level positions in accounting and finance. Some of the available courses include accounting information systems, organizational behavior, and professional responsibilities. To receive the bachelor’s, students must earn 120 credits. By completing additional coursework (30 extra credits, totaling 150), students are eligible to sit for the CPA exam. Another option is to take advantage of one of Hiram’s articulation agreements with Ohio Northern University of Case Western Reserve University and pursue a master’s of accountancy at one of those two institutions. Students finish with a required senior seminar/capstone experience to reflect on key things learned while studying for their degrees.

Distance learners looking for accounting schools online find a bachelor’s in business administration-accounting at the University of Massachusetts-Amherst’s Isenberg School of Management. The program prepares students to work in accounting, tax, consulting, and auditing positions. Faculty members who teach on campus also provide online instruction, and all professors have professional experience in accounting. Topics covered include information systems, taxation, financial reporting, and managerial accounting. Required major courses span from CPA law to cost accounting. Accounting majors also complete 11 core business courses like business data analysis, corporate finance, and management communications. Students must complete 120 credits in order to graduate, with at least 45 taken at UMass Amherst. Distance learners benefit from academic advisors with an average of 10 years experience helping online students and a university career center dedicated to helping graduates land jobs at global accounting firms.

McKendree University in Lebanon, Ill., offers an online accounting degree through its School of Business. The bachelor’s of business administration-accounting concentration program is designed for busy working professionals. Online courses last eight weeks, and most elearners take two classes at a time. Students in the online 120-credit bachelor’s program can choose to apply their credits toward the 150 credits required to sit for the CPA exam, and may earn the additional 30 credits needed by taking extra undergraduate or graduate courses at McKendree. Transfer students appreciate the school’s generous transfer credit policy: only 30 of the required 120 credits must be taken at McKendree, so up to 90 transfer credits with a grade of ‘C’ or better may be accepted from regionally-accredited institutions. At least 40 credit hours must be taken at the 300 or 400 level. Accounting courses offered include business ethics, taxation of business entities, and auditing.

Students interested in online accounting degree programs find a bachelor’s of science in accounting at Northwest Christian University in Eugene, Ore. The degree is designed for people already working at the entry-level in accounting, and includes coursework in marketing and management, as well as accounting. Northwest’s flexible program includes six start times each year, eight-week sessions, and an optional one-night-per-week in-person class. Students may get credit for prior learning experiences in the military, law enforcement, or on-the-job training. Prerequisites for the major include principles of accounting I and II, microeconomics, and macroeconomics. Required classes cover auditing, accounting for nonprofit organizations, and small business management. Students also take a capstone and an internship, providing real-world experience in the accounting field. Graduates leave the program prepared for accounting-related positions in government, professional CPA firms, not-for-profit groups, and corporations.

Northwood University-Michigan offers distance learners a BBA in accounting . Based in Midland, Northwood is a university focused on the business professions and one of the best online colleges for accounting. The private, nonprofit school serves more than 23,000 students annually. The BBA in accounting degree curriculum blends accounting and management courses, and students start taking accounting classes their first semester in the program. Classes are available in either eight- or 16-week sessions. The 123-credit, four-year bachelor’s degree program requires courses like accounting ethics, estate planning theory, philosophy of American enterprise, and strategic planning. Students interested in an intensive, accelerated program of study may be eligible to earn both their bachelor’s and master’s in just four years total, with the four-year BBA/MBA track. In this fast-track program, students earn their bachelor’s degree in three years and then spend the fourth year working on their master’s in business administration.

One of the best online colleges for accounting is California Baptist University, which offers distance learners a bachelor’s of science in accounting . Located in Riverside, CBU is a private, Christian school with a liberal arts focus. The accounting program prepares students to sit for the CPA exam and to begin entry-level accounting work in numerous industries. CBU’s online professors have real-world experience in accounting, and small class sizes give distance learning a more personal touch. In addition to general education requirements, students in the BS in accounting program must complete 73 units within the accounting major. The curriculum explores financial analysis, government accounting, quantitative business analysis, and information systems. During their senior year, students take a zero-credit pass/fail exit exam covering basic business concepts. Students interested in gaining more experience outside the standard curriculum have the opportunity to participate in a two-week summer program in England at Oxford University.

Distance learners searching for online accounting degree programs find a bachelor’s of science in accounting at the Carson College of Business at Washington State University. Located in Pullman, WSU is a public research school serving close to 30,000 students each year. The degree stands out for its emphasis on technology and intellectual innovation. WSU’s flexible online format features seven-week courses, and students can attend either part- or full-time. The curriculum is available entirely online, with no on-campus requirements. Each learner works with a special online advisor to plan out their program of study. To earn the BS, students must complete the university’s general education requirements, which can be satisfied with a direct transfer degree, as well as business and accounting courses. The program requires a six-credit international experience , designed to ensure that students find success in the global marketplace. Numerous options exist for fulfilling the requirements, including coursework, study abroad, and international internships.

Campbell University in Buies Creek, N.C., offers an accounting degree online. Campbell is a private university with an enrollment of more than 6,000 undergraduate and graduate students. The BBA in accounting offers the curriculum completely online, with no in-person requirements, and eight-week accelerated semesters allow students greater flexibility and the ability to graduate more quickly. The program prepares students for work as CPAs in environments including government, corporations, and nonprofit groups. The degree provides a broad background in general principles of business, and is considered especially good preparation for students who want to try entrepreneurship. The 124-credit bachelor’s of business administration requires 42 general education credits and 78 within the accounting concentration, and students must maintain a minimum 2.0 GPA to graduate. Transfer students may receive credit for up to 64 semester hours from a regionally-accredited institution. Accounting courses at Campbell vary, from philosophy of business to management information systems to corporate finance.

Students can earn an accounting degree online at the University of Mary in Bismarck, N.D. The private Catholic and Benedictine university makes it a point to include these religious and ethical viewpoints in all courses. The BS in accounting , offered through the Gary Tharaldson School of Business, is designed for busy working adults, and can be earned completely online or in a hybrid online/face-to-face program. Students learn numerous business principles and how to apply accounting practices in various settings. The curriculum includes courses like Internal Revenue Service (IRS) regulations, auditing standards, and SEC reporting requirements. The program’s accelerated format means students have greater flexibility and can complete their degrees more quickly than possible in a traditional on-campus bachelor’s program. Students can choose to continue on in the MBA in accountancy program or to pursue additional credits in order to be eligible to take the CPA exam.

Liberty University in Lynchburg, Va., is one of the best online colleges for accounting. Liberty is a private, non-profit research university with a Christian focus and an enrollment of more than 110,000, making it the largest Christian university in the world. Distance students can earn a bachelor’s of science in accounting entirely online with no on-campus or residency requirements through Liberty’s School of Business. Tuition is the same regardless of where students live. Featured courses in the program include international business, accounting ethics, taxation, and governmental and not-for-profit accounting. Liberty’s 120-credit BS degree allows transfer students to get credit for up to 90 credit hours of previously completed coursework. Thirty credit hours must be completed at Liberty. All Liberty students must complete three courses (12 credit hours) on Christian life and thought. Graduates find accounting-related work in numerous industries, including government, education, corporations, and non-profits.

Students looking for accounting schools online find a bachelor’s of science in accounting available at Point Park University in Pittsburgh. The fully online program gives students practical accounting skills and an understanding of key subjects like macroeconomics, using computers in accounting, and business law. All online instructors have years of real-world experience in accounting, and focus on providing students with the skills needed to succeed professionally. Graduates of the program are prepared to work in entry-level jobs across multiple industries in positions like tax preparer, internal auditor, government accountant, and financial analyst. The 126-credit degree takes two to four years to complete, depending on previous coursework transferred in to the program. Each course lasts eight weeks and tuition is charged as a flat fee per credit, regardless of where students live or what their residency status may be. The curriculum features courses including management science, micro computing, and corporate finance.

Mount Mercy University is regarded as one of the top private universities in Iowa. MMU not only boasts one of the lowest tuition rates among four-year institutions in the region, but also offers a number of accredited distance education options, including a fully online bachelor of business administration . The online business degree prepares students for entry-level employment or graduate school through synthesizing theoretical knowledge with hands-on experience. MMU’s online bachelor’s degree in business consists of at least 123 credits in general education, major, and elective coursework. Student takes classes in fundamental business topics like international marketing, organizational behavior, and employment law and labor relations. Online classes are presented asynchronously and usually last 10 weeks, though students may opt to take certain courses in an accelerated five-week format. Though students are not required to log into their class at any set times, they are expected to contribute to discussion through forums and group presentations.

Located in the vibrant city of Milwaukee, Wisconsin Lutheran College enrolls 1,200 students annually in its traditional and distance education programs. The school offers an online bachelor of science in business management and leadership catered to adult learners who want to accelerate their career by learning from experts in the field. Students engage with their peers through collaborative assignments. Major coursework includes human resources management, leading organizational change, and business diversity. This online business degree is offered in an accelerated format, enabling students to earn their diploma in as few as 21 months if they have the necessary general education and prerequisite requirements. Distance learners also gain access to professional development tools through the Student Success Center. Each student gets a personal academic advisor. Additionally, WLU offers a flat and affordable online tuition rate for the 48 credit hours it takes to complete major requirements.

Asbury University provides accessible higher education to working students through its full-accredited weekend and distance education pathways. The school offers an online bachelor of science in organizational management for students interested in pursuing a business leadership role. This 124-credit program prepares graduates for management positions in such fields as supply chain and information technology. Students complete major courses in personal finance, principles of marketing, and economics. The degree plan also contains 46 elective hours, enabling distance learners to pursue advanced business topics or complete a minor concentration. With several entry dates throughout the year and classes structured in eight-week formats, students can start their degree and finish it quickly. Asbury provides a flat tuition rate for all online learners and offers financial assistance, including scholarships and discounts for military personnel. Additionally, the school allows students to transfer up to 75 credits from prior college-level work.

Located just 35 miles northwest of Pittsburgh in Beaver Falls, Pennsylvania, Geneva College enrolls 1,700 students annually and boasts an average class size of 17. The school’s online bachelor of professional studies in management consists of 36 credits of major coursework in topics like marketing, quantitative business analysis, and principles of management and supervision. Through a curriculum grounded in practical skills development and knowledge of best business practices, students prepare for leadership roles as hospitality managers, real estate brokers, and account executives. Geneva’s online business degree program requires at least 120 credits to complete. The school has a flexible transfer policy, and with the right amount of prior credits, students can graduate in as little as 16 months. Distance learners also have access to Geneva’s financial aid, including loans, scholarships, military discounts, and employer reimbursement.

King University offers degree completion tracks for distance learning students who have completed prior college work and want to earn their diploma in as little as 16 months. Consisting of 48 semester hours of major coursework, the online bachelor of business administration focuses on developing the critical thinking and cooperative decision-making skills students will need to obtain leadership positions in the field. In addition to general education and prerequisite classes, students must complete major classes in areas such as corporate finance, information systems, and statistics and business research methods. They must also successfully pass a comprehensive assessment during their final semester. Students enjoy one of the lowest per-credit tuition rates of any business administration degree online program. Students may transfer up to 76 credits to further lower the cost of their degree. Courses are offered year-round, allowing students to enter the program at their convenience.

LeTourneau University is a leader in distance education. The school provides 90 academic programs to over 2,700 students annually, including an online bachelor of business administration degree . Whether a student’s goal is to obtain a job upon graduation or pursue graduate degree work, LeTourneau’s BBA prepares them through a curriculum that combines general leadership skills with specific major topics. Classes include business ethics and strategic management. Additionally, the university assists online students with internship support and opportunities to join professional organizations like the Sigma Beta Delta, the national business honor society. LeTourneau stands out among other online business degree programs because it offers two concentration options for BBA students. The first is a generalist track, ideal for students who are unsure of their career goals but want to get a well-rounded education. The second option enables online learners to specialize in human resource management, taking coursework in advanced areas like staffing, employment law, and compensation and benefits.

Sage College was founded in 1916 as a school committed to helping women become active, equal members of society. Today, The Sage Colleges continue the tradition of empowering students by providing a variety of accessible academic programs, including an online bachelor of business administration . Professionally accredited by the International Assembly for Collegiate Business Education, this BBA degree trains students to communicate effectively and make decisions responsibility. Major coursework for this business administration degree online program includes topics like management information systems, business law, and applied statistics. Students must also take 12 hours of guided electives or use these credits to complete either an organizational studies or marketing concentration. Additionally, to give students the most comprehensive education available, an internship and a capstone seminar in the major are also part of the degree requirements. TSC offers an affordable tuition rate for all online students regardless of where they reside.

Located in Gainesville, the University of Florida offers a large number of degree programs through its online learning platform, including a bachelor of science (BSBA) and a bachelor of arts (BABA) in business administration . Both degree paths teach students fundamental skills such as applied statistics and strategic marketing, preparing them for such careers as budget analysts, fundraisers, and labor relations specialists. The BSBA, with its focus on advanced areas like entrepreneurship and real estate, is suited for students who want a more intensive business education. The BABA provides flexibility, enabling students to pair a core business education with another speciality field such as sports management or mass communications. Though tuition for all online students is affordable overall, Florida residents enjoy a steeply discounted rate. Students may begin the program in fall, spring, or summer. Students transferring into this business degree online program should have at least 60 credits with a minimum 2.0 GPA if they want to be competitive.

Recognized as one of the top private institutions of higher learning in Oregon, Corban University enrolls over 2,000 students worldwide through its distance education programs. Among these offerings is an online business degree program that is both comprehensive and flexible enough to prepare students for careers or graduate studies in a wide variety of fields. The 129-credit degree plan includes core classes in financial management and business law. Students then declare a concentration, choosing either marketing, accounting, or leadership and management. Additionally, learners can apply their remaining 23 elective hours towards advanced business seminars, a complimentary minor, or any other course at Corban. Corban’s per-credit tuition rate is the same for all online students regardless of residency. Financial aid, including loans, grants, military benefits, and employer reimbursements, is available. Oregon residents get access to additional state-only scholarships.

Houghton College offers both an online bachelor of science (BS) or bachelor of business administration (BBA) in management . Both degrees train students in core business practices such as strategic accounting, employee recruitment and training, and labor laws. The BS offers more flexibility in terms of degree planning, allowing students to pursue separate interests, while the BBA is structured and focused, ideal for professionals in a business field looking to advance their career. Houghton’s online classes are asynchronous with no required log-in time. Additionally, students do not take standard tests, instead learning through applied methods. Courses are offered in eight-week terms and there are six start dates every year. Students with 63 transfer credits can earn their online business degree in just 18 months.

Located near Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, Eastern University serves over 3,500 students every year in its traditional and accelerated distance education programs. The online bachelor of science in business administration accepts new students every seven weeks and enables them to earn the degree entirely online or through a hybrid delivery method. The degree plan totals 121 credits and includes major courses in organizational behavior, research methods, and ethical principles of management. Students must complete a capstone project in organizational change, in which they conduct independent research and present their findings and proposed solutions. Tuition for this business administration degree online program is priced at a flat per-credit rate for all students. Distance learners are eligible for financial aid, including institutional scholarships, partner grants, and military benefits. Eastern also provides academic services and professional resources, including personal advising, resume assistance, and networking opportunities, through its Office of Talent and Career Development.

Mount Vernon Nazarene University offers a number of distance education programs through its School of Graduate and Professional Studies, including an online bachelor of business administration . This accessible degree allows students to maintain their professional and personal obligations while learning the financial and management skills necessary to become responsible leaders. Students can choose to earn a standalone BBA or complete a specialization in finance, human resource management, marketing, or general management. Application requirements for MVNU’s business administration degree online program include an online form, all educational transcripts, and a GPA of at least 2.0. Prospective students must also have a minimum of 56 transferable credits from a regionally-accredited college or university. MVNU guarantees its tuition rate will not increase as long as students enroll in classes regularly.

Baker University was founded in 1858 as the first university in Kansas. Today, Baker enrolls over 3,200 students annually. Baker’s online bachelor of business administration in management consists of 42 major credits, with courses including business analytics, leveraging technology in decision making, and best practices in management and leadership. Students also take on coursework in their area of concentration, choosing from either human resources, finance, or healthcare administration. All distance learners enjoy the same affordable tuition rates, priced per-credit based on the level of the class. Over 98% of Baker students receive financial aid in the form of scholarships and grants. The university also allows students to earn college credit from previous academic work, professional training, and life experience via assessment.

Evangel University offers over 100 academic programs to 2,200 students every year, many of which are accessible to distance learners. The online bachelor of business administration in management teaches students to set business goals and then develop the strategies to successfully meet them. The major curriculum totals 70 credits and includes classes in business ethics, organizational design, and compensation and benefits. Students also learn critical thinking and effective communication skills. This degree prepares graduates for careers in such fields as event management, production design and logistics, and nonprofit administration. To apply for this online business bachelor’s degree program, prospective students need to submit official SAT or ACT scores as well as their official academic transcripts with a minimum GPA of 2.0. The same low online tuition rate is available to all distance learners regardless of residency status.

Endicott College’s online bachelor of science in business management caters to working professionals through flexible scheduling and hands-on skills application that builds on previous experience. The 126-credit degree plan includes coursework in major areas like organizational behavior, applied research methods, business communications. Students may choose from a concentration in either homeland security or trauma studies. Additionally, degree candidates must complete a senior thesis that synthesizes all they’ve learned in the program. Seniors must either complete new research or propose solutions to pertinent challenges in the field. Distance learners receive professional support including advising, resume building help, internship resources, and career development opportunities. Online tutoring and around-the-clock technical support are also available. Applicants to this online bachelor’s degree in business program need to submit a 500-word statement of career goals.

Misericordia University provides part-time and accelerated programs on its Denver, Colorado campus as well as online via the Jenzabar platform. Included in the distance education options is a bachelor of science in business administration . The online business degree allows students to greatly personalize their degree plan to align with future academic and career goals. Totalling 121 credits, students take classes in core topics such as international business, finance, and information systems before using the ample number of elective hours to pursue advanced studies. The Misericordia online business degree emphasizes hands-on skill acquisition. Distance learners can receive a wealth of internship and professional development resources through the Insalaco Center for Career Development. Online classes are structured in 7½ week sessions and there are eight entry dates every year. Many part-time students earn their degree in a little over two years.

Keiser University’s online bachelor of arts in business administration teaches students fundamental business concepts as well as modern topics such as international economics and the effects of globalization. The curriculum totals 120 credits, with core classes in financial management and entrepreneurship. Students also choose a concentration in management, international business, transportation and logistics, marketing, finance, or human resource management. Degree students must complete an integrated studies capstone course, a project-based seminar that consists of independent research and presentation of findings. Courses for this online business degree program are asynchronous, enabling students to view course content and complete assignments with a great deal of flexibility. Keiser provides an affordable tuition rate for all distance learners regardless of where they live. Financial assistance is available, including awards like the Florida Resident Assistant Grant.

Located in Indiana city, Huntington University boasts over 70 undergraduate academic programs, many of which can be accessed online, including a bachelor of science in business administration . Totaling 128 credits, this degree emphasizes the quantitative skills necessary to make effective decisions in the ever-changing business landscape in the U.S. and abroad. Major courses include such topics as operations management, organizational behavior, and public relations. Degree candidates must also complete a capstone project of original scholarly or professional work that addresses a real issue in the field. Additionally, students have 37 elective hours with which to pursue advanced studies in business or to complete a complimentary minor or double major. To apply to this business administration degree online program, prospective students must submit an online application, a personal statement, and all relevant academic accreditation and test scores. Huntington provides an affordable tuition rate to distance learners, financial aid resources, and a generous transfer policy that allows students to gain college credit from work experience.

North Greenville University is a private liberal arts institution that services 2,500 students annually through its 50 campus and distance academic programs. The online bachelor of business administration in leadership and management teaches students skills in project management, team building, and motivation. Core requirements include classes in organizational behavior, applied leadership, and business and report writing. Additionally, students may elect to pursue a marketing minor. The NGU program emphasizes hands-on learning; students must complete 12 credit hours of internship as well as create a personal portfolio their senior year. In addition to personal academic advising, NGU also offers resources such as career planning, counseling, and student advocacy. All online business degree students, regardless of where they reside, enjoy the same affordable per-credit tuition rate. Financial aid is available and includes loans, military discounts, and state grants.

Located in Saint Paul, Minnesota, Bethel University offers 35 online academic pathways, including a bachelor of science in business management . Designed for working professionals, this degree program consists of 51 credits in the major, including core classes like foundations of human resource management and corporate finance. Advanced coursework differs depending on whether a student chooses the general management or global business concentration. All degree candidates must complete a business capstone experience in which they propose a solution to pertinent client needs within an organization. Most students earn their diploma in 16 to 21 months. Applicants must hold have at least 60 prior college credits in order to be eligible for Bethel’s online business degree program. Tuition is priced at the same per-credit rate for all distance learners.

The College of Saint Scholastica is an innovator in distance education, providing continuing education classes called Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs) and fully online degree options. The 128-credit bachelor of arts in management prepares students for leadership roles through coursework in management ethics, information systems, and the principles of marketing. CSS offers a concentration in the rapidly growing field of healthcare management. Additionally, students may choose to take MOOCs (in ethical leadership, for example) free of charge to supplement their degree work. The online business degree program at CSS is designed with flexibility in mind. Students may enroll in one or two classes a semester and still be eligible for program services and financial aid. Online courses are structured in accelerated eight-week sessions, enabling students to earn their degree in less than three years even with no transfer credits.

Considered one of the top public institutions of higher education and research in the U.S., Rutgers University enrolls over 58,000 students annually in its campus and distance education degree programs. The 120-credit online bachelor of arts in business administration features core business classes in business communications and marketing principles as well as major courses in financial statement analysis and the management of multinational businesses. Degree candidates must complete a capstone project in business policy and strategy. The final project prepares students for entry-level positions or further education at the graduate level. Students can enter this business administration degree online program at three points during the year. All distance learners have access to financial assistance. To enroll, applicants must have a at least 18 credits from previous college work with a 2.5 GPA minimum.

Through its partnership with Thomas Jefferson University, Philadelphia University offers 14 fully-accredited distance education degree programs, including a bachelor of science in business management . The degree plan totals 120 hours and includes major courses in topics like operations management, economic decision making, and evaluating statistical data. One-third of the curriculum is dedicated to electives, which students may transfer in from prior college work or take at PU. Students must also complete a capstone seminar. The program prepares graduates for careers as sales managers, marketing research analysts, and business owners. Online classes are offered in eight-week sessions and are asynchronous. Most students earn their online business degree in two to three years, depending on how many credits they transfer in and the rate at which they complete coursework. PU stands out as one of the few universities that has actually reduced its tuition rate over time.

Located in Saint Louis, Missouri, Webster University caters to working professionals and other nontraditional students through its distance education platform. The online bachelor of of arts in management focuses on strengthening the relationship between supervisors and employees within a business environment. The degree plan totals 128 credits and includes 39 hours of coursework in topics like business technologies, marketing, and descriptive statistics. Degree candidates must complete a senior capstone experience in managerial policies and strategies. The curriculum also includes a large number of elective hours which students can use to pursue a complimentary minor at Webster or fulfill with transfer credits. Admission materials include relevant academic records and standardized test scores. Freshman applicants must also write a personal statement and provide at least one letter of recommendation.

The online bachelor of arts in business administration at Hope International University builds on students’ prior education and experience. In addition to core classes in fundamental areas like international business and organizational ethics, students may also choose to complete a sport business management concentration. At least one internship experience is also required. Distance learners may coordinate with the university to complete an internship in their local community or over the summer through a designated HIU partner. The program prepares graduates for leadership positions in private businesses, nonprofits, and government organizations. HIU supports its students through a number of services, including personal academic coaching, career development, and networking opportunities. Students can complete the online business degree in just three years.

Located in Longview, Texas, LeTourneau University prides itself on providing a strong education rooted in Christian values. This reputable institution offers a BS in computer information systems for working students who need the flexibility of a completely online degree. A degree in computer information systems is similar to a degree in computer science, but focuses more on practical skill development. Students learn about computer networks, data management, and computer system design. Classes include software engineering, web development, and programming for information systems. LeTourneau’s online classes are structured in an accelerated five week format. Transfer students must possess at least a 2.0 cumulative GPA and grades of C or higher. Transfer students must take a minimum of 18 major credits at LeTourneau. As a private institution, LeTourneau charges the same tuition rate for in-state and out-of-state students.

Founded more than 130 years ago by a Catholic congregation, Duquesne University is rated a top tier university by U.S. News & World Report. The school serves over 9,000 students while maintaining a low 14 to 1 student-to-faculty ratio. Duquesne’s BS in computer systems technology focuses on solving practical technology issues. Courses include information technology for modern organizations, web technologies and applications, and technical communication and writing. Students also take a course in social, ethical, and legal issues in computing technology in order to gain a comprehensive understanding of the field. The school accepts admissions on a rolling basis. New students may begin in the spring or fall terms. Transfer students must have a 2.5 cumulative GPA and grades C or higher. Duquesne accepts 60 credits from previous two-year schools, and up to 90 credits from previous four-year schools. Transfer students must complete the last 30 credits at Duquesne. Duquesne prides itself on providing extensive resources for its online students, including an online learning support center, an online writing center, and an online library. Out-of-state students pay the same tuition as their in-state counterparts.

With 97% of its students receiving financial aid, Lewis University strives to make education affordable for all learners. Although it is a Catholic university that teaches Lasallian values, Lewis serves students of all faiths and backgrounds. The school’s accelerated online bachelor’s degree in computer science allows students to graduate with their degree in a short amount of time. Students may choose between a bachelor of arts or a bachelor of science. Topics include programming languages, cybersecurity, and artificial intelligence. The eight-week online schedule allows students to complete their studies faster than usual. Only transfer students qualify for this degree option. Applicants must possess at least 30 transferable credit hours and hold a cumulative GPA of 2.0 or higher. Students with prior programming and coding experience may earn credit for prior learning. In-state and out-of-state students pay the same tuition rate.

Located in Chicago’s business district, DePaul University serves students from all 50 states and 114 countries. The award-winning school has been ranked one of the best national universities by U.S. News & World Report and one of the best Midwestern colleges by the Princeton Review. DePaul’s online bachelor’s degree in computer science is a degree completion program for students who have already completed prior college coursework. Notable courses include software development, database systems, and applied computing. Seniors complete a capstone software project. Incoming students must provide proof of a minimum of 45 quarter hours or 30 semester hours of transfer credit, including one semester of C++ or Java. Applicants must also hold a cumulative 2.0 GPA. The school grants scholarships to students that demonstrate academic excellence, school involvement and leadership, and a dedication to serving others. In-state and out-of-state tuition for this online bachelor's in computer science degree are the same.

The University of Illinois at Springfield promotes student engagement through volunteerism, student committees, NCAA athletics, clubs, and civic outreach. The school offers an online BS in computer science and an online BS in information systems security . Students in the computer science online degree take core coursework in operating systems, data structures and algorithms, and programming languages. Learners may choose electives such as HTML design, intrusion detection, and internet in American life. The online version of this program requires 30 previous college credits and a minimum 2.0 cumulative GPA. DePaul accepts new online students every fall and spring semester. Distance learners may complete internships in their hometowns for academic credit. All online students qualify for a low e-tuition rate.

Consistently ranked one of the nation’s best universities, the University of Florida conducts cutting-edge research in every discipline. UF students and faculty have made breakthroughs in battling brain cancer, detecting gravitational waves, and protecting Florida’s environment. Despite its prestigious status, the school ensures its education is affordable; over half of UF’s students graduate with no student loan debt. The online BS in computer science at UF emphasizes technical knowledge and communications skills. Students learn programming basics and complete coursework in digital logic and software engineering. Transfer students with less than 59 hours of previous credit must hold a 2.5 cumulative GPA. Additionally, they must have taken analytic geometry and calculus 1. Students with 60 or more transferable hours must have passed analytic geometry, calculus 1, 2, and 3, and physics 1. Applicants need a C or higher in prerequisite courses in order to be competitive.

Located in Riverside, California Baptist University uses the latest educational technologies to deliver high-quality online education. The BS in computer information technology focuses on solving technical and computer problems in organizations. Unique courses offerings include legal and ethical practices in information technology and e-commerce systems development. Since CBU built its institution on Christian values, all students must take overview of the Bible and foundations of Christian thought. Transfer students must have a minimum of 24 prior credits and a 2.0 cumulative GPA.The average length to graduate from this program is approximately 16 months. Courses to begin every 8 weeks, allowing students to finish their program quickly. In-state and out-of-state students pay the same tuition rate.

City University of Seattle offers programs in eight countries, including China, Canada, and Mexico. The bachelor of science in applied computing from CityU gives students hands-on experience in tech. Distance learners develop skills in Python, information security, and systems analysis and design. The program requires a capstone project at the end where students demonstrate the knowledge they learned through examinations, written work, and oral presentations. CityU maintains a year-round schedule and a rolling admissions policy. Applicants must show proof of a cumulative 2.0 GPA. This degree requires 180 credit hours for students to graduate. Students may transfer a maximum of 135 transfer credits towards this degree. Prior learning assessments give students with practical the chance to apply their knowledge for school credit. Students coming from out-of-state do not pay higher tuition.

Located in Denver, Regis University offers a completely online BS in computer science . Interesting course offerings include ethical leadership in computer science and computer architecture. A class in intelligent systems gives students a solid foundation in artificial intelligence technology. Students in this major must complete 128-133 credit hours, depending on general education credits and if a minor is chosen or not. Since the school is Jesuit, online students must take religion courses. Regis allows a maximum of 90 transfer credits from a recognized college, as long as student earned a C- or higher. Regis also grants credits for related work experience. Out-of-state students pay the same in tuition as their Colorado counterparts.

The BS in computer science from Oregon State University is a unique post-baccalaureate degree. All students already hold a bachelor’s degree, and the program is tailored for students who want to develop technical skills on top of their current credentials. This program offers one-year, two-year, three-year, and four-year tracks so students can fit their studies into their schedules. The accelerated one-year program is designed for those individuals who can commit to a full-time online course load. This post-baccalaureate computer science online degree does not require any experience or education in computer science, only a bachelor's degree in any field. Students may start in any of the four terms, except summer if they apply for the one-year track. Students must exhibit excellent time management skills and dedication to succeed.

Located in Norfolk, Virginia, Old Dominion University offers more than 100 programs, including online and distance learning options. The online BS in computer science heavily emphasizes coursework in science and mathematics. Degree seekers take interesting courses, such as computers in society, principles of programming languages, and cryptography for cybersecurity. Graduates have the skills to excel in technology careers, including software engineering, database development, network administration, and system design. This online computer science degree requires students to complete at least 30 credits at ODU. Depending on the course, professors may require students to meet online, turn in assignments, and take exams at certain times. New students may begin the program in spring, summer or fall.

California University of Pennsylvania offers an interdisciplinary BS in computer information systems program to students interested in business technology. Distance learners take courses like visual programming, decision support systems, web programming, and concepts in enterprise resource planning. In their last two semesters, students apply what they learned by creating a real-world computer information system project for a company or organization. Credit hours obtained through local internships give students hands-on experience within the field. Classes for this online computer science degree begin every fall and spring semester. Asynchronous online courses allow students to complete coursework on their own schedule. Full-time students can complete the program in as little as 24 months. Applicants with backgrounds in college algebra, trigonometry, business, or pre-calculus fit well with this program. Transfer students must show proof of a 2.0 or higher cumulative GPA and good academic standing from previous schools.

Charleston Southern University offers a BT in computer science for students who hold associate degrees in approved technical fields. This online bachelor's in computer science degree consists mostly of upper level classes that prepare students for a successful career in technology. Since each student comes from a different background, faculty members work with each individual to create a program that fits their schedule and education. Transfer students beginning with the maximum of 89 hours often graduate within two years. Associate degrees that fit best with this program include engineering technology, computer technology, and telecommunications management. Prior to enrollment, prospective students must complete a college math course and an in-person programming class in C++ or Visual Basic. Students need a minimum of 125 credit hours to graduate.

Park University, located in Missouri, offers a BS in information and computer science degree that gives students the skills to pursue graduate studies or professional tech positions. Students choose a specialization in one of five areas: computer science, software development, networking and security, information technology, or data management. Courses like writing for online environments, user interface design, and computer architecture educate students in the fundamentals of technology. Depending on their chosen focus, students can pursue careers as applications programmers, systems analysts, information technology specialists, database analysts, web programmers, or software engineers. Students in this online bachelor's in computer science program take Cisco Certified Network Associate (CCNA) courses that prepare them for the CCNA certification exam. Transfer students need a cumulative 2.0 GPA or higher to be considered for admission. Students from out-of-state pay the same in tution as their in-state counterparts.

Founded as a Christian high school in the 1920s, Concordia University Texas maintains strong ties to the Lutheran church. The school offers a BA in computer science for working students looking to pursue a career in software design or computer programming. Students benefit from the school’s location in Austin, a technology hub with ample internship opportunities. This online computer science degree requires students to complete a minimum of 128 credits to graduate. CTX boasts a strong Christian foundation and requires all students to pass religion-based courses. Transfer students need a minimum cumulative 2.0 GPA if transferring between 12 and 83 credits or a 2.5 GPA if transferring less than 12 credits. Out-of-state students pay the same as Texas residents. The Texas Transfer Scholarship offers students $2,000 in financial aid if they graduate with an associate degree in the state or qualify for the Texas Core Complete program.

Located in Gaffney, South Carolina, Limestone College is the largest private college in South Carolina. It's a small school, with only 1,000 traditional day students and 2,500 extended campus students. The completely online BS in computer science, computer and information systems security focuses on securing private data for businesses, government agencies, and institutions. Courses include cryptology and access control, continuity and disaster recovery planning, and security architecture and design. Students must complete a capstone project, which consists of a large-scale project that draws on knowledge from previous courses. Classes are structured in eight-week sessions with six terms each year. Students take two courses per term. Transfer students who have an associate degree from a South Carolina technical college do not have to complete general education requirements. All other transfer students may bring a maximum of 66 credits from a two-year school or 92 credits from a four-year college. Out-of-state students pay the same tuition as in-state students.

The oldest Catholic university in Florida, Saint Leo University has instructed students for more than 125 years. The school began offering online courses in the 1990s. SLU offers a BS in computer science with a specialization in information assurance to students interested in forensics and internet security. Distance learners take courses in artificial intelligence, disaster recovery, penetration testing, and network design. Transfer students may apply up to 64 credits to their degree. SLU holds an agreement with more than 40 community colleges across the U.S., allowing associate students to easily transfer into the program. SLU requires students to complete 120 credits to graduate. Since Saint Leo is a private school, in-state and out-of-state tuition are the same.

Fort Hays State University's Virtual College offers students a low cost alternative to in-classroom learning from a reputable institution of higher education. The school provides more than 50 online degree options, including an online BS in computer science . Distance learners may choose a concentration in business or networking. Business classes include managerial finance and management, while networking classes include advanced routing and interwork troubleshooting. This online computer science degree prepares students for fulfilling careers such as computer engineer, software engineer, network architect, and web developer. FHSU's partnership with the Kansas community college system allows qualified students to easily transfer courses to FHSU. Course formats may be asynchronous or synchronous. Students must complete 120 credits in total in order to graduate.

Located in Madison, South Dakota, Dakota State University boasts a high employment rate for graduates, with a 94% employment rate for the latest class and a 100% employment rate in certain majors. DSU’s online bachelor’s degree in computer science gives students access to the newest technologies so they can stay ahead of their peers. Courses include assembly language, object-oriented design, and database management. Graduates often pursue careers as programmers, software engineers, or tech startup entrepreneurs. DSU schedules on-campus and online courses at the same time, so both student groups have the opportunity to collaborate with each other on projects. All computer science professors have a Ph.D. This program requires transfer students to have 24 credit hours or more. Applicants must hold a cumulative GPA of 2.0 or higher.

Located 45 minutes from New York City, Mercy College offers more than 90 undergraduate, graduate, and certificate programs. The school promotes education for underrepresented groups such as minorities and first-generation students. Mercy Online’s BS in computer science combines theoretical technical knowledge with hands-on skills. Courses include forensic and electronic discovery, artificial intelligence, and network security. Mercy requires students to obtain 120 credits in order to graduate. The college accepts up to 75 transfer credits from a two-year school or 90 credits from a four-year school. Mercy prides itself on offering affordable tuition compared to other private institutions in the area. No matter state of residence, all students pay the same tuition. Outstanding transfer students are eligible for scholarships.

Located in Columbus, Ohio, Franklin University designs all of its programs for busy, working adults. As a result, the school offers dozens of flexible online programs. Franklin’s online BS in computer science is language-independent, meaning students’ skills stay relevant even if their primary coding language becomes obsolete. Notable classes for this online computer science degree include human-computer interaction, object-oriented analysis and design, and mobile programming. This degree requires students to complete 124 credits in order to graduate. Franklin accepts a maximum of 94 transfer credits. Distance learners must complete a final capstone project in order to demonstrate their knowledge and technical skills. All students pay the same tuition regardless of their state of residency.

Florida Institute of Technology boasts impressive alumni, including astronauts, the first female four-star general, and CEOs of Fortune 500 companies. Florida Tech’s online BS in computer information systems prepares students for careers in the technology industry or private businesses. Core courses include electronic commerce, internet applications, and business computer skills. Courses are delivered asynchronously so students to have the freedom to complete work at their own pace. Students learn through discussion boards, chat rooms, videos, and interactive media. Florida Tech requires students to complete a total of 124 credits to graduate. Transfer students may receive credit for classes in which they earned a C- or higher. Out-of-state students pay the same tuition as Florida residents.

Located in West Haven, Utah, Independence University has provided distance education for 37 years. The school was founded in 1891 and holds accreditation from the Accrediting Commission of Career Schools and Colleges. IU's BS in software and mobile applications development degree teaches students how to design, code, and test software. Courses include computer law, solution concepts, webpage programming, and programming concepts. Graduates pursue careers such as mobile app developer, web developer, IT entrepreneur, and software developer. Without an elective requirement, students can complete this degree in as little as 36 months. IU requires learners to complete 180 credit hours minimum to graduate from this major. IU provides all students with a new tablet at the start of the program, and a laptop during the fourth module to keep upon graduation.

Located in Fayette, Missouri, Central Methodist University prides itself as a four-year liberal arts college affiliated with the United Methodist Church. Distance learners at CMU can complete an online BA or BS in computer science . Courses include scientific computer programming, web page design, mobile application development, and a capstone seminar. Students learn how to examine and solve a variety of software issues, such as flaws in the code. Courses last either eight or 16 weeks. Transfer students must have a 2.0 cumulative GPA to apply. This online computer science program requires a minimum of 124 credits. Out-of-state students pay the same in tuition as their in-state counterparts.

Located in La Jolla, California, National University offers more than 100 distance education programs, including an online bachelor’s degree in computer science . Core requisites for this online computer science program include computer ethics, programming languages, and digital logic design. The program features a strong foundation in calculus and science. Students complete labs in which they create and test their own code. Online classes take place over a one month period. National holds agreements with many California community colleges, making it easy to transfer credits. The school offers a Transfer to Success Scholarship that awards up to $5,000 to transfer students who come from a community college in California, Nevada, or an approved out-of-state program. Qualifying students for this scholarship must already have 30 credits with a minimum cumulative GPA of 2.0.

Rated 19th best northern university by U.S. News and & World Report and one of the best northeastern colleges by the Princeton Review , Rowan University is nationally recognized for its academic excellence. The main campus is located in Camden, New Jersey, just across the river from Philadelphia. Rowan’s BA in law and justice features coursework in international terrorism, law and human rights, and theories of crime and criminality. Rowan places students in internships with a criminal justice agency or social services. Rowan’s online criminal justice degree prepares you for a career in law enforcement, security services, court services, corrections, or human services. Students must complete 120 credits to graduate. Transfer students must take a minimum of 30 credits at Rowan University. Most students finish in four years, but the program can be completed in three. Applicants should have a minimum GPA of 2.0 and they must also have at least 24 hours of transferable credits or an associate degree. Tuition is the same for in-state and out-of-state students. Alumni of partner schools receive a 15% tuition discount.

Located in Bristol, Tennessee, King University offers a bachelor's of science in criminal justice . The degree can open doors to careers in child and family law, state and federal law enforcement, and in the prison and probate systems. King offers a course on restorative justice that focuses on repairing and restoring relationships between victims and criminals. Students must complete 48 credits in the major with a total of 124 credits to graduate. Students can complete the degree in as few as 16 months. The online program has specific start and end dates and is not self-paced. To apply for the major, students must have 45 hours of prior college coursework completed. All students must pass a general education exam before graduating from King and receiving a diploma.

Norwich University is well-respected in both the military and in the public sphere. The school ranks as one of the top online schools in the nation, according to U.S. News and World Report . The online bachelor of science in criminal justice gives students the flexibility to attend class and complete coursework at their own pace each week. Students can pursue a concentration in intelligence and security management or choose electives that support their desired career path. Students conduct field work and complete a capstone project that highlights legal and ethical issues in the field. Norwich offers three program start dates a year. Applicants must have 30 credits of prior college work to be accepted into the major. Students with significant college coursework may transfer up to 90 credits. Upon review, admissions will consider military training and achievements for credit.

Dallas Baptist University takes a Christ-centered approach to education. All students receive a well-rounded liberal arts education coupled with leadership and service to Christ. DBU’s online bachelor of arts and science in criminal justice features coursework in homeland and organizational security, multicultural perspectives, and juvenile delinquency. Students participate in interactive online classes comprised of chat rooms, emails, and discussion threads. Students must complete 120 credit hours to graduate from the online criminal justice program. For students interested in transferring into DBU, at least 30 credits must be completed at the school with a minimum of 12 credits specific to the major. To graduate, students must have a cumulative GPA of 2.0 or higher.

Keiser University located in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, offers an online criminal justice bachelor degree at more than 20 campus locations in Florida and online. Students take exciting courses, such as victimology and deviant behavior. The criminal justice curriculum at Keiser prepares students for careers in the court system, law enforcement, and private security. Students must finish 120 credits to complete their degree. There are no prerequisites for entering the major. For transfer students, the school accepts an associate degree or previous credits from regionally accredited schools. Students who attended non-regionally accredited schools will need their class credits evaluated by a faculty member for potential approval.

In 2017, Thomas Jefferson University and Philadelphia University merged to create Jefferson. The history of this academic institution spans more than 300 years. Students work together in an educational atmosphere inside and outside of the classroom environment. Their online program takes into account the ever-changing job market and worldwide marketplace. Jefferson's online BS in law enforcement is designed for students who want leadership roles in law enforcement and corrections. Students take classes in police administration and planning for law enforcement organizations. Jefferson accepts students on an ongoing basis and offers several times throughout the year. Most students complete the BS in law enforcement in two to three years. The degree requires distance learners to complete 120 credit hours. Each course lasts eight weeks.

Located in Macon, Georgia, Mercer University is rated one of the best 382 colleges by the Princeton Review . The online criminal justice leadership degree covers classes in forensic behavior, victimology, and cybercrime. Mercer’s program features field-based learning so that students can apply classroom study with hands-on experience. Prerequisites in this major include introductory classes in psychology and sociology. Mercer requires 120 credits to graduate. The university runs on semesters and online students follow a similar calendar as on-campus students. Mercer accepts transfer credits from regionally accredited schools. Applicants should have a 2.5 GPA or higher. Mercer offers on-campus information workshops for interested students as well as online webinars for distance learners.

Buena Vista University in Storm Lake, Iowa offers an online bachelor’s degree in criminology and criminal justice . Graduates from this program seek jobs in the judicial system, criminal justice system, and in law enforcement. Classes in criminal justice include criminal justice systems, modern social problems, judicial process, and criminological theory. Online classes start every eight weeks. BVU accepts transfer students with an associate degree or 60 credit hours from an accredited school. Admission staff also consider CLEP exams and life and work experience for credits. BVU provides students with financial specialists that can help them calculate costs of tuition and choose which program is the best for their lifestyle. Applicants are encouraged to work with an advisor when going through the application process. Students will continue to work with an advisor throughout their schooling to plan a course of study to complete the program.

Indiana Wesleyan University is a Christian college nestled in the Indiana town of Marion. The 100% online bachelor of science in criminal justice degree includes courses in forensics, constitutional law, probation and parole, cybercrime, and restorative justice. Classes meet once a week. Professors have both professional and academic experience. The online bachelor’s in criminal justice culminates in a capstone project based on an ongoing challenge in the modern-justice system. Students must have 60 credits before starting the degree. IWU offers a bridge program students that have 36 to 59 credits but have not already completed an associate degree. The program can be completed in approximately 18 months.

Located in Brooklyn, St. Joseph’s College offers a comprehensive online BS in criminal justice practice and policy . Students receive a well-rounded liberal arts education as well as in-depth courses in law and criminal theory. Notable classes include crime analysis, criminology, and race, class, and gender in criminal justice. Students learn from expert criminologists and apply their knowledge with hands-on experiences. The school conducts rolling admission so students can be accepted year round. The online criminal justice degree requires 120 credit hours for completion. St. Joseph's accepts up to 90 transfer credits.

Hampton University’s accelerated BS in criminal justice allows students to complete their bachelor’s degree in a short amount of time. Students in this major take diverse classes such as homeland security, terrorism & national security, and forensics crime scene investigation. The program requires 120 hours to graduate. Students who complete an approved internship receive three credits towards their major. Classes run on an eight-week schedule and students can take up to six credits per cycle. Students do not have to attend class at a specific time, but must adhere to assignment submission dates. Timed exams are taken online and submitted directly to the professor. Most courses do not require students to visit campus.

The criminal justice online degree at Loyola University is designed for current criminal justice professionals seeking greater responsibility in their organizations. The BA in applied criminal justice leadership features coursework in investigation management, advanced criminal procedure, organizational leadership in criminal justice, and conflict management. Since the program was created for working professionals, classes are asynchronous so students can complete coursework on their own schedule. Classes are offered in five eight-week sessions per year. Transfer students can apply 15 credit hours from an approved school towards their major. In order to receive transfer credit, students must have received a GPA of 2.0 or above. Any required class meetings are scheduled for the evenings or weekends.

Penn State’s World Campus serves nearly 18,000 online students. Established in 1998, the World Campus offers 150 online certificate and degree programs. Penn State’s online bachelor of science in criminal justice has received numerous accolades, including the prestigious Academy of Criminal Justice Sciences (ACJS) certification. Distance learners take courses in ethics in criminal justice and alternatives to incarceration. Students have the option to add a minor in homeland security by completing an additional 18 credits. This minor provides students with more job opportunities due to the increase in terror concerns. To graduate, students must complete 120 credits. Transfer students must earn 36 of the final credits at Penn State. In order to receive credit for previous college courses, students must have earned a “C” grade or above and hold a 2.0 GPA.  All credit transfers from other colleges are subject to approval by the academic admissions.

The online criminal justice degree program at Southwestern Assemblies of God University in Waxahachie, Texas, was created in 2006 after an increase in demand for jobs in the field. The BA in criminal justice prepares students to pursue careers as homicide detectives, FBI agents, crime scene investigators, DEA agents, or state troopers. What sets SAGU apart from other schools is its unique approach to blending criminal theory with a biblical point of view on justice. SAGU offers an experiential learning credit program for students with relevant work experience. Students can earn up to 45 credits by demonstrating their expertise and knowledge during a three-hour course. Online programs are more affordable than on-campus classes. SAGU requires students provide evidence that supports they are born-again Christians living a biblically based lifestyle.

Located in Kentucky, Midway University offers a 100% online bachelor of science degree in criminal justice . This program features a strong foundation in psychology, with students taking courses in forensic psychology, psychopharmacology, and abnormal psychology. Students also complete coursework in criminal organizations, interviewing, and terrorism. The college employs faculty with real-world criminal justice job and career experience. Students must complete 120 credit hours to graduate. The University Career Readiness Service Center assists students with finding job opportunities. The center also helps students research additional qualifications they may need to obtain those positions. Midway offers competitive tuition costs and payment plans. Courses follow an eight-week schedule with five start dates each year.

Founded in 1878, Ashland University has a long history of providing students with a strong liberal arts education. The online bachelor’s degree in criminal justice instructs students in both theory and practical skills. Students take courses in criminal profiling, gangs and cults, ethics, and the role of police in modern society. Classes are offered in eight-week cycles.
