Registered nurses receive the training they need to assume roles as advanced practice leaders with several programs such as the 72-credit DNP in adult-gerontology acute care nurse practitioner. Another option, the 43-credit DNP in executive leadership, trains nurse executives with expertise in business, finance, and entrepreneurship. Nursing programs may require clinicals that students complete in northern Virginia, Maryland, or Washington, D.C. Online programs in engineering and occupational therapy round out the portfolio of doctoral-level e-learning options.

Keiser University operates campuses across Florida, providing learners with career-focused education that employers value. Keiser's fully online and hybrid programs emphasize flexibility for working professionals. The school's doctoral online programs cover several disciplines, including nursing, criminal justice and criminology, and curriculum and instruction. Business professionals enroll in the doctor of business administration (DBA) to hone leadership skills and learn practical approaches to address contemporary business challenges. Faculty combine theory, applied research, and hands-on problem-solving. Students choose one of three DBA specializations: management, marketing, or global business. Another business option, the Ph.D. in industrial and organizational psychology, provides a research-based curriculum that trains business psychologists who understand how to use advanced psychology to enhance business outcomes. Other e-learning options include the Ph.D. in curriculum and instruction and Ph.D. in educational leadership. Online students can expect affordability and personalized attention from faculty with industry expertise.

One of the nation's top Christian colleges, Regent University offers a comprehensive catalog that emphasizes both rigor and a Christian worldview. The Virginia Beach college offers online doctoral programs in areas such as divinity and theology, psychology, and nursing. The 36-credit doctor of ministry unlocks participants' leadership potential while empowering them with effective theological and discipleship tools needed for ministerial work. The 56- to 64-credit Ph.D. in communication and 49-credit doctor of strategic communication both prepare specialists for high-level positions in the private and public sectors. Each requires a residency, which faculty may conduct online. Regent also offers a portfolio of doctoral degrees just for educators seeking to assume leadership roles. These include the Ed.S. in educational leadership-curriculum and instruction and Ph.D. in education - Christian education leadership. Other degree options for doctoral students include the Juris Doctor and Psy.D. in clinical psychology. Students pay the same per-credit tuition regardless of residency.

Located in Lynchburg, Virginia, Liberty University is one of the world's largest Christian colleges. For more than 30 years the school has offered distance learning, addressing the needs of modern learners with a robust portfolio of degrees. Liberty's online doctoral programs include fields such as history, education, divinity, and business. The 60-credit DBA, accredited by the Accreditation Council for Business Schools and Programs, offers 15 specializations, including Six Sigma and international business. The education unit offers several options, including the Ph.D. in special education and Ed.D. in curriculum and instruction with various tracks. Other notable options include the Ed.D. in traumatology, which students may use to acquire counseling licensure. Faculty use eight-week sessions to deliver online programs. Students can access tutoring, live webinars, an online library, and assistance from Liberty's academic advisors. In addition to competitive tuition, students pay the same per-credit rate regardless of in-state or out-of-state residency. Qualified military personnel enjoy generous tuition discounts.

Texas Tech's foray into distance learning began in 1966 when four "flying professors" brought an engineering program to 78 students. Today, faculty deliver about a dozen doctoral programs using a fully online format or a hybrid option with some classes on campus in Lubbock. Five fully online doctoral programs focus exclusively on education. The 63-credit Ph.D. in curriculum and instruction offers tracks in STEM; curriculum studies and teacher education; and language, diversity, and literacy studies. The hybrid 96-credit Ed.D. in educational leadership prepares school executives who can deploy research-based solutions to effect instructional change at the preK-12 level. Other education degrees include the Ed.D. in agricultural education and Ed.D. in higher education administration. A 72-credit Ph.D. in systems and engineering management uses a hybrid format to cultivate experts ready to lead in industry, academia, and consulting. Nonresidents enrolling solely as online learners get a big break with comparable in-state tuition rates.

The University of Alabama's location in Tuscaloosa belies the storied institution's international appeal. One of the nation's top public colleges, UA offers online doctoral degrees through Bama By Distance in fields such as education and healthcare. The hybrid 72-credit Ed.D. in educational leadership emphasizes the application of research-based administrative best practices in K-12 school environments. The 66-credit Ed.D. in higher education administration uses a hybrid cohort format to train administrators who specialize in leading postsecondary institutions. Senior engineers enroll in the Ph.D. in aerospace engineering and mechanics, which is delivered primarily online. This 72-credit option requires that engineers contribute to the field's existing body of knowledge through their research dissertation. The hybrid Ed.D. in instructional leadership educates a new generation of nurse educators. Social workers enroll in the cohort-based doctor of social work program in preparation for roles in academia or agency management. Online learners pay the same per credit tuition regardless of residency, making this an affordable school for e-learners.

One of the nation's leaders in online education, the University of Florida offers seven online doctoral degrees focused mostly on education and nursing. The Ed.D. in higher education administration blends online learning with immersions held 2-3 weekends per semester at UF and at other colleges in the state. This option requires part-time enrollment. Students graduate in about five years. The cohort-based Ed.D. in curriculum and instruction diverges from the administrative role to focus on educational technology. Faculty train a cadre of scholars who, through their unique research, identify ways to optimize the use of educational technology in classrooms. Students in this 54-credit program benefit from a tight-knit online community of practice that offers small group mentoring to ensure participants' success. The 35-credit MSN-to-DNP cultivates nurses equipped to apply evidence-based practices in their profession. The Ph.D. in classical civilization; Ph.D. in Latin and Roman studies; Ed.D. in educational leadership; and Ed.D. in teachers, schools, and society complete the portfolio.

Established as an agricultural and mechanical school, Mississippi State University has grown into a comprehensive world-class institution. Online students join the Bulldog family through a suite of online doctoral programs in engineering and education. The college, located in Starkville, offers Ph.D. programs in chemical engineering and industrial and systems engineering. Students interested in a customizable curriculum enroll in the Ph.D. in instructional systems and workforce development. This program builds on participants' work and educational experience as it prepares them for roles as faculty, researchers, evaluators, or consultants. The Ph.D. in community college leadership provides a more specialized curriculum as faculty prepare educators who can innovate two-year colleges. Participants learn how to incorporate best practices, pursue continuous improvement, and improve and implement programs at rural community colleges. Online students pay in-state tuition regardless of their residency. Student services include online tutoring and career services to help learners successfully chart their careers.

Shenandoah University operates campuses across Northern Shenandoah Valley and Northern Virginia. Shenandoah, affiliated with the United Methodist Church, offers a select number of online doctoral degrees that focus exclusively on healthcare. RNs keen on advancing their careers choose one of two points of entry into a doctor of nursing program: the 71-78 semester-hour BSN-to-DNP or the MSN-to-DNP. Both offer family nurse practitioner and psychiatric mental health nurse practitioner concentrations. Students complete core BSN-to-DNP classes at Shenandoah's Winchester campus or at the Inova Center For Personalized Health in Fairfax. Faculty offer DNP classes online. The 30-credit doctorate in occupational therapy enrolls registered occupational therapists who want advancement to roles as leaders and evidence-based scholars. Shenandoah manages a strong pharmacy program with three doctoral degrees, including a dual pharmacy/public health option. The 16-credit doctor of physical therapy enrolls practicing clinicians with a master's in physical therapy or another healthcare program. Shenandoah's close proximity to the Washington, D.C., metro area gives healthcare professionals access to one of the nation's most prosperous regions.

A pioneer in distance learning, Illinois gives domestic and international students access to more than 90 online programs taught by renowned faculty. Faculty design doctoral degrees to accommodate the needs of busy educators. Illinois' online doctoral degrees include the 64-credit Ed.D. in diversity and equity in education, an option that focuses on methods to champion social justice in P-16 learning environments. The innovative Ed.D. in education policy, organization, and leadership offers a concentration in learning design and leadership. The 64-credit curriculum suits instructional designers and others who want to advance their proficiency in the design, evaluation, and management of e-learning programming in educational environments. This Ed.D. also offers a concentration in global studies in education for educators who want expertise in the globalization of pedagogy and policy in different contexts. Unlike other online doctoral programs at Illinois, the 64-credit Ed.D. in human resource development focuses on adult learning, developing experts in training and development, workplace learning, and performance improvement within organizations.

The University of South Carolina celebrates almost 220 years of academic excellence and leadership as the state's flagship college. UofSC builds on a tradition of innovation with online doctoral programs in education and engineering. The 60-credit Ed.D. in educational practice and innovation offers four specializations: curriculum studies, STEM education, learning design and technologies, and education systems improvement. Faculty deliver the program using eight-week sessions. Nonresident Ed.D. students pay nominally more per-credit tuition than their in-state peers. The department requires neither the MAT nor GRE for admission to the Ed.D. Computer engineering students immerse in applied learning to complete the 60-credit Ph.D. in computer engineering. Participants design, develop, and test new computer hardware, and engage in research initiatives and projects sponsored by government agencies and other entities. Engineering faculty offer two other online Ph.D. programs: mechanical and nuclear engineering. Admission to the engineering programs requires GRE scores.

Andrews University is an Adventist college located in Berrien Springs, Michigan. Students at all levels receive instruction that blends scholarship and practical Christianity. Andrews' e-learning platform enables learners to earn an online doctorate in healthcare, leadership, or education. The 64-credit doctor of science in physical therapy prepares learners for Certified Manual Physical Therapist credentials, and offers two specializations: women's health or orthopaedic manual therapy. Alternatively, the online doctor of physical therapy's 35- to 38-credit curriculum provides advanced training to practicing physical therapists. Mid-career leaders can optimize their skills and advance credentials with either the 54-credit doctor of leadership, which culminates in a project, or 60-credit Ph.D. in leadership, which requires a dissertation. Educators seeking to advance into leadership roles acquire the necessary training when they complete either the Ed.D. or Ph.D. in higher education administration. Students who want to maintain a focus on classroom instruction enroll in either the Ed.D. or Ph.D. in curriculum and instruction.

Omaha, Nebraska-based Creighton University consistently ranks among the nation's best schools for online learning. The Jesuit school offers online doctoral programs that cover business, healthcare, and education. The 54-credit DBA offers a hybrid curriculum accredited by the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business. It blends classroom instruction and applied research to optimize students' managerial and leadership skills. Creighton manages a strong nursing program that culminates in the DNP. Faculty provide a practical curriculum that develops highly skilled and compassionate nurses. The hybrid curriculum offers multiple DNP tracks and the flexibility for working nurses to proceed at their own pace. The 143-credit doctor of pharmacy, also hybrid, prepares experts in patient data collection, pharmacy care planning, patient counseling, and medication therapy management and delivery. The 60-credit Ed.D. in interdisciplinary leadership offers an adaptable curriculum suitable for participants with eyes on senior positions in education, business, or other fields. All online students pay the same per-credit tuition regardless of residency.

Purdue University is a tier-one public research college well-known for producing innovators and inventors, including 25 astronauts and many scientists. Doctoral students join the Boilermakers when they pursue e-learning options in education, nursing, technology/computer science, and law and legal studies. The Ph.D. in higher education and Ph.D. in education leadership and policy studies each require 90 credits that students complete using a hybrid delivery format. RNs acquire the credentials needed to perform at the height of their profession when they complete a fully online 48-60-credit DNP. The four-year Juris Doctor program requires participants complete 92 credits on a part-time basis. Successful students qualify to sit for the State Bar of California exam. The 90-credit doctor of technology offers a fully online curriculum infused with STEM education and opportunities for world-changing research. Online students enjoy free software they can access from anywhere. Purdue students pay the same per-credit tuition regardless of whether they live.

A top research college, The University of Alabama in Huntsville enrolls about 10,000 students on campus in Huntsville and through e-learning. Online doctoral programs focus on nursing and engineering. RNs choose between the DNP or Ph.D. in nursing. Working nurses complete the 40-credit DNP in as few as 28 months of full-time online enrollment, developing a repertoire of administrative and systems leadership competencies highly prized by employers. Alternatively, the 69-credit Ph.D. in nursing prepares nurse scholars who can advance the field through their research focused on rural and medically underserved populations. The engineering department offers four 69-75-credit Ph.D. options: industrial, systems, civil, and engineering management. Though students complete the engineering degrees primarily online, faculty require some in-person sessions. Doctoral students may transfer up to 24 credits of relevant coursework into the engineering degrees. All eligible UAH students may use scholarships and fellowships to help fund their education.

Founded in 1839, the University of Missouri is an e-learning powerhouse with more than 150 online degrees and certificates. The school offers online doctoral programs in architectural studies and nursing. The Ph.D. in architectural studies graduates professionals with expertise in designing environments for today's world. The 72-credit program offers emphasis areas such as design education, sustainable design, facility management and design, and housing and neighborhood design. Students complete the degree completely online with an optional on-campus workshop held in June. The nursing school's 72-credit DNP offers three emphasis areas: family nurse practitioner, pediatric clinical nurse specialist, and adult-gerontology clinical nurse specialist. While faculty deliver the DNP online, enrollees must attend on-campus sessions throughout the program. The length of the DNP depends on whether RNs enter the part-time program with a bachelor's or master's degree. Known as a military-friendly institution, Mizzou offers personnel, veterans, and families a tuition reduction to make doctoral degrees more affordable.

The University of the Cumberlands' two campuses serve students residing in the Appalachian Mountain regions in several states. Online learners earn degrees through the growing virtual campus. Faculty design all graduate programs for at-your-pace learning to accommodate busy schedules. The virtual campus offers fully online doctoral degrees in several fields, including IT, business, and counseling. The 60-credit Ph.D. in information technology focuses on predictive analytics to develop experts in big data, cybercrime security, and smart technology. Business faculty offer the 66-credit DBA, an applied degree for working professionals seeking to lead and innovate in business environments. Students interested in a more research-focused option enroll in the 66-credit Ph.D. in business, which prepares scholars and researchers for academia and the business arena. The 66-credit Ph.D. in counselor education and supervision trains scholars to teach counseling programs and senior administrators of behavioral health agencies. Other programs include the Ph.D. in leadership and executive Ph.D. in business. Cumberlands offers one of Kentucky's lowest tuitions.

World-renowned for strong academics, the University of Wisconsin Madison lauds many accomplishments, including 19 Nobel Prize winners. Students who cannot attend classes in person can use the UW e-campus to earn one of 60 online graduate degrees. Online doctoral degrees cover education and nursing. Students interested in either secondary or postsecondary education leadership enroll in the three-year Ed.D. in career and technical education. Faculty use a hybrid cohort format to deliver a 60-credit program that focuses on leadership, the effective use of data, partnerships with business and industry, and faculty and staff development. Another option, the 54-credit Ed.D. in sustainability development, offers a fully online curriculum that allows learners to explore sustainability in one or more contexts, including transformative learning and systems thinking. RNs can earn a practice-focused DNP or research-to-practice-focused Ph.D. in nursing from one of the nation's top nursing schools. RNs can enter either program with a BSN or MSN for even more flexibility.

school_name: A T Still University of Health Sciences
description: A.T. Still University opened the world's first osteopathic medical school in 1892. The college trains physicians and healthcare administrators who specialize in a whole-person approach to medicine. ATSU enrolls about 3,000 students annually and operates campuses in Missouri and Arizona. Through a virtual campus, online learners earn one of several doctoral programs in health education, health science, and administration. The doctor of health administration prepares students for effective industry leadership. The three-year degree focuses on topics such as healthcare law, health policy, medical care economics, and health information systems. The Ed.D. in health professions immerses students in experience-based learning designed for practicing clinicians, healthcare leaders, educators, and similar professionals. Ed.D. coursework covers topics such as educational program evaluation, students assessment, and transformative leadership and ethics. A third program, the doctor of health sciences, develops effective managers who excel in organizational leadership, evidence-based standards, and applying research to professional practice.

One of the nation's top Catholic colleges, Pittsburgh-based Duquesne University blends academic rigor and the school's Spiritan values. Duquesne's online doctoral programs focus on education and healthcare. The 54-credit Ed.D. in instructional technology ideally suits professionals who want to specialize in infusing technology into teaching and learning in academic and corporate settings. The nursing school offers the most online doctoral programs at Duquesne. RNs choose a 35-credit DNP in clinical leadership or 35- to 62-credit DNP in executive nurse leadership and healthcare management. RNs who prefer a research-focused degree choose the 56-credit Ph.D. in nursing, which develops nurse-scientists primed for careers in research, nursing education, or organizational leadership. A one-of-a-kind program, the 47-credit Ph.D. in nurse ethics blends nursing courses and healthcare ethics and includes a study abroad component. All professional nurses receive up to a 55% tuition discount. Duquesne does not require GRE scores for admission to any of its online doctoral programs.

A historically black institution, Hampton University is a liberal arts college with a strong emphasis on science and the professions. HU, located in Virginia, offers online doctoral programs in business, education, and nursing. The 61-credit Ph.D. in business administration equally benefits MBAs and those from non-business fields who want to advance into leadership roles. While faculty deliver courses online, all students complete two summer residencies on campus. The Ph.D. in education management uses an accelerated 66-credit curriculum to cultivate skilled innovators who can lead higher education or preK-12 education institutions. The 64-credit Ph.D. in counselor education and supervision enrolls licensed counselors who want to build on their practice while building clinical and supervisory skills. The 48-credit curriculum of the Ph.D. in nursing focuses on family and family related research. Nursing students can supplement required courses with elective and cognate courses of interest. Online students pay the same tuition regardless of where they live, making these affordable options for nonresidents.

Rowan University offers a partially online psychology degree. The hybrid bachelor's in psychology program requires 120 credits, 30 of which must be complete at the Glassboro, New Jersey, campus. The program is ideal for working professionals who need convenience and flexibility, and full-time students can finish the degree in four to five semesters. Coursework explores topics including social psychology, research methods, and learning and behavior. Graduates enter careers in areas such as social work, human resources, and marketing. Applicants must have an associate degree or at least 24 transferable credits with a 2.0 GPA. Tuition is the same for in-state and out-of-state students, and Rowan offers start dates in the fall and spring.

TFC is a Christian liberal arts institution that prepares students for personal and professional service. Founded in 1907, the Georgia college offers an online BS in counseling psychology that requires 126 credits. In addition to psychology classes, students pursuing the psychology degree online complete coursework in Bible and theology, humanities, and social sciences. While online learners have the flexibility to complete coursework according to their schedule, on-campus programs and activities — such as sports and student government — are open to online students. Online resources include advising, tutoring, career counseling, libraries, and information technology assistance. All online students pay the same tuition rate, regardless of residency. Students who enroll in at least six credits may be eligible for financial aid.

Corban offers one of the best online psychology degree programs in the country. Students learn the fundamentals of psychological theory. Located in Salem, Oregon, Corban helps students unite their career and their Christian faith. Students earning Corban’s bachelor’s in psychology study topics such as community agency counseling, juvenile delinquency, abnormal psychology, and family violence and dysfunction. Psychology students can pursue a concentration in family studies, which prepares them for careers in social work and ministry, or pre-counseling/clinical, which prepares students to enter a graduate counseling or psychology program. Applicants should demonstrate faith in Jesus Christ and a Christian lifestyle and should have strong writing abilities and transferable credit with at least a 2.0 GPA. Applicants should also have at least one year of work, college, or life experience after graduating high school.

LeTourneau offers an accelerated BS in psychology that emphasizes counseling techniques rooted in the Christian faith. Intended to prepare students for graduate studies or to enter the workforce, this online bachelor’s degree in psychology explores theoretical fundamentals and builds students' analytical abilities and understanding of human behavior. Students complete 42 major credits in courses such as personality theory, abnormal psychology, learning and cognition, and physiological psychology, along with 50 credits of general education courses such as English composition and college algebra. Students also complete required theology and vocation classes. Graduates with an online bachelor's degree in psychology from LeTourneau pursue further education and enter professions such as social work and the ministry.

Located in Redding, California, Simpson University offers one of the most rigorous and cheapest online psychology degree programs. Simpson's bachelor’s in psychology is available through its Adult Studies division, ASPIRE, which provides mainly degree completion programs. The psychology degree can be completed in 12 to 16 months. Students earning Simpson’s online psychology degree learn about modern psychology, including fundamental theories, research techniques, and practice methods. Students complete 12 courses in the major; each course lasts five weeks, and students enroll in one class at a time. In addition to coursework, the program requires a capstone portfolio project. Online students have access to academic and career counselors. Applicants must submit a statement of purpose, a reference letter, and college transcripts.

Located in Bristol, Tennessee, King offers accelerated programs with year-round courses at an affordable price. King's BS in psychology degree completion program is available fully online to accommodate the schedules of busy adults who are balancing professional, personal, and academic commitments. Graduates pursue careers in areas such as business and social services. Coursework builds students' understanding of psychological theory while strengthening critical thinking, communication, writing, and research skills. The program comprises 124 credits, 48 of which are in major coursework. Most students complete the program in about 16 months. To graduate, students must pass a comprehensive general education exam, the Outcomes Assessment at King. Applicants must have earned at least 45 previous credits. All online students pay the same per credit tuition rate, regardless of residency.

Keiser's BA in psychology teaches students to apply the scientific method, understand the complexities of ethical research on human and animal subjects, and recognize the connection between neuroscience and behavior. Keiser’s diverse curriculum, which includes courses in sports psychology and forensics, prepares students for careers in mental health, law, social services, and education. This accredited online psychology degree offers four tracks: human services, business, healthcare and fitness, and developmental psychology. Students take a single four-week class at a time. Students can complete all classes online or blend online and on-campus instruction. Keiser supports active military members, veterans, and military families by offering scholarships, funding, and college credit for military training through the Yellow Ribbon Reintegration Program.

Endicott College offers a BS in psychology that emphasizes research and human behavior. After earning their online degree in psychology, graduates pursue careers in a variety of fields, including law, education, and business. Students examine human behavior from infancy to old age as they learn to assess research and explore methods such as survey design and psychological observation. The 126-credit program offers specializations including early childhood education, homeland security, and Montessori education. Endicott’s four-year internship program helps students gain practical experience while earning their degree. The internship program is likely a main reason that most Endicott students secure employment within one year of completing their bachelor’s degree.

school_name: Northwest University-College of Adult and Professional Studies
description: Founded in 1934, Northwest is a Christian institution in Kirkland, Washington. The university prepares students to enter the workforce while also nurturing their spiritual well-being. Northwest’s BA in psychology integrates studies in anthropology, cultural areas, and sociology. Students pursuing this online, accredited psychology degree explore social and cultural injustice under the supervision of skilled faculty. In addition to completing 120 credits of coursework, students gain hands-on experience during required practicums. Applicants must submit official college transcripts; although Northwest prefers applicants with at least a 3.0 GPA, those with a 2.7 GPA may be admitted on a probationary basis. All online students pay the same tuition rate, regardless of residency.

Huntington University's BS in psychology is both rigorous and affordable. The standout online psychology degree is ideal for working professionals planning to change or advance their career. Huntington instruction is rooted in the Christian faith. Students with at least 40 college credits can pursue Huntington's accelerated online psychology degree to graduate more quickly. Students without previous college experience can earn an associate degree from Huntington to meet the psychology program prerequisites. Huntington also awards credit for work experience. Psychology classes emphasize interpersonal relationships, counseling fundamentals, research methods, and human development to prepare students for graduate school or the workforce. Employees of Parkview Health receive discounted tuition.

DBU offers convenient, flexible, and affordable degree completion programs. The Christian university, which emphasizes both faith and academics, offers a bachelor's of arts and sciences in psychology . The 120-credit program includes 30 major credits. Students examine topics such as personality, human growth and development, and marriage and family. Students must complete all coursework with at least a 2.0 GPA to graduate. Most online students take six to nine credits per semester, but each student determines their ideal schedule by consulting with an academic adviser. The university accepts an unlimited number of transfer credits from regionally accredited four-year schools and up to 66 transfer credits from accredited two-year schools.

Students in TUW’s online bachelor’s in psychology program explore human behavior to support mental health. The curriculum aligns with APA guidelines, which demonstrates its quality and rigor. TUW's psychology program provides students with fundamentals necessary for graduate studies. While earning TUW's online psychology degree, students develop an understanding of experimental, developmental, learning, and abnormal psychology. Each course lasts eight weeks, and in-state and out-of-state students pay the same per credit tuition rate. Full-time students complete the 120 required credits in three and a half years, and most part-time students require about seven years. TUW accepts transfer credits, which can shorten completion time. Applicants must hold a high school diploma or equivalent.

Located in Plymouth, Wisconsin, LU serves nearly 3,500 students and is affiliated with the United Church of Christ. The university offers a bachelor’s in psychology that builds students' understanding of scientific inquiry methods and strengthens their ability to observe and articulate the role of the brain in behavior, thought, and emotion. Coursework examines the social, biological, and cognitive capabilities of organisms as they adapt to their environments. This accredited online psychology degree requires 48 major credits and 24 credits in areas such as brain, body, and mind. Students can participate in an internship to fulfill elective requirements, during which they apply classroom studies under the guidance of a site supervisor and a faculty member. Graduates pursue further education and enter careers in areas such as counseling and social work.

Students in the online BA in psychology program at DeSales learn to understand behavior and mental functions through careful examination of humans and animals. Graduates enter mental health professions, forensic-related careers, and graduate school. The 120-credit program includes classes in psychopathology, psychobiology, and research design and analysis. Online coursework may be asynchronous, synchronous, or a combination of the two. DeSales accepts up to 75 transfer credits and awards credit for College Board advanced placement exam scores, military training, and portfolio assessments. Students can test out of certain classes after demonstrating mastery of the subject matter by passing an exam and completing additional requirements, such as a presentation or paper.

BVU offers an online bachelor’s in psychology degree completion program. The 33-credit online degree in psychology emphasizes behavior and cognitive functions of both humans and animals. Through a research-based approach, students are prepared for further studies or to enter careers in the mental health and criminal justice fields. Graduates secure positions such as marriage therapist, family counselor, and law enforcement officer. Classes examine topics such as sensation and perception, stereotypes and prejudice, and sleep and dreams. Applicants to BVU's degree completion program should have completed an associate degree or 60 credits at a regionally accredited institution. BVU may award credit for college-level exams and life experience. Applicants should submit college transcripts; while not required, an interview is strongly encouraged.

Penn State offers two online psychology degrees: a BA in psychology and a BS in psychology . Students gain analytical and critical thinking skills necessary to understand interpersonal interactions. As two of the best online psychology degree programs in the country, Penn State's programs prepare students to enter careers in fields such as business, education, law enforcement, and healthcare. These interdisciplinary degrees include liberal arts coursework in humanities and social sciences. While the BA program focuses on social work and counseling courses, the BS emphasizes psychology in the workplace. Students earning the BS degree can pursue a life science track, which includes coursework in anthropology and biology and is ideal for students interested in graduate school or counseling careers. Penn State students can join the school's fully online psychology club, which comprises more than 100 members from around the world.

A member of the Women’s College Coalition, Bay Path offers an online BA in psychology that builds students' knowledge in behavior and mental processes. Students learn to apply psychological theories, observe human behavior, and draw conclusions using analytical skills. Graduates pursue careers in education, public relations and marketing, and social science research. Bay Path offers its online psychology degree through The American Women’s College, which meets the demands of busy women with accelerated, six-week sessions. The curriculum is similar to that of many online psychology degree programs; coursework covers communications, child development, and mathematics. Students also explore health, counseling, and social psychology. Bay Path offers six start dates per year and accepts up to 90 transfer credits. Students can earn credit for military, work, and volunteer experience.

Located in Elgin, Illinois, Judson University offers a bachelor’s in applied psychology that covers the theories and methodologies used to address human behavioral problems. This online accredited psychology degree prepares students for careers managing others in a variety of fields. Course requirements include statistics, psychological measurement, lifespan development, and faith issues. The 46 required core credits develop problem-solving skills, explore how social identify influences values and beliefs, and improve spoken and written communication skills. Judson students undertake research projects, during which they assess and disseminate findings. Students also complete a supervised internship at an organization of their choice.

TIU, a Christian-based university, offers an online BA in psychology that encourages students' understanding of others and self. Graduates are prepared to enter the workforce or pursue an advanced degree. Coursework explores psychological theory and research methods and equips students to analyze human behavior and help people navigate life’s complexities. The program requires 11 core credits, which include courses in research design and statistics. Students complete a three-credit integrative course that examines psychology and Christianity. Students can pursue an emphasis in counseling psychology. Applicants must have a high school diploma or equivalent and at least a 2.0 GPA in all high school and college coursework.

A private, liberal arts institution, SMWC is the oldest Catholic college in Indiana. Founded as a women’s college, SMWC has admitted men since 2015. The bachelor’s in psychology prepares students to support others' mental health through data collection, analysis, and interpretation. Students build research skills during required classes in developmental and biological psychology. In addition to 34 core credits, students earning a psychology degree online from SMWC complete electives that explore topics such as ethics in helping professions and counseling techniques. Graduates pursue advanced degrees and secure positions such as mental health caseworker and social worker. Applicants must have a high diploma or equivalent and must submit ACT or SAT scores and college transcripts.

SAGU is a Christian institution that promotes both spiritual and professional development. The university offers an online bachelor’s in psychology , a pre-professional degree that blends studies in the field of psychology with the Christian faith. A fully online degree in psychology, this bachelor’s prepares students to become mental health professionals. The available minor in addictions prepares students to become licensed chemical dependency counselors. Some graduates pursue careers in the the ministry, business, or law. Courses encourage students and faculty to exchange ideas about the integration of biblical beliefs and psychology. Applicants must demonstrate commitment to a Christian lifestyle and must submit a Christian character reference, high school and college transcripts, and placement scores.

A Cambridge, Massachusetts, institution with a history of training educators, Lesley University offers an online bachelor’s in psychology for students interested in human behavior, brain functions, and social processes. As one of the country’s best online psychology degree programs, the bachelor's prepares graduates for careers in fields such as mental health and business. Courses cover topics such as personality, memory, individual development, and culture. Students explore psychological theories and approaches. In addition to coursework, students complete a supervised internship, during which they gain hands-on experience. While the program is open to most U.S. students, residents of Georgia, Kentucky, Oregon, Washington, and Nevada are ineligible to apply.

SDC's online bachelor’s in psychology is ideal for working professionals. Highly regarded among online psychology degrees, the program integrates topics from a variety of disciplines, such as education and business, to help students identify connections between psychology and other subjects. The program requires 123 credits, all of which are available fully online, and each class lasts five weeks. Full-time students typically complete the program in four years. Coursework explores human development and concepts in morality and spirituality. Research and service learning opportunities provide practical experience and allow students to apply classroom knowledge. Students can pursue a minor or emphasis in a complementary field, such as business administration.

Located in Kentucky, Midway offers a fully online bachelor’s in psychology that prepares students to work as teachers, market researchers, and social workers. The program also provides the foundation required to succeed in graduate programs. All faculty members are doctoral-level clinicians and offer students one-on-one attention throughout their studies. Midway's bachelor's program is one of the highest quality yet cheapest online psychology degrees in the country. Students learn fundamentals in psychology through courses examining human growth, multicultural psychology, and learning and cognition. Of the 120 required credits, 30 comprise major credits. Students also complete a community service practicum. Applicants must submit ACT or SAT scores, have at least a 2.5 high school GPA, and rank in the top half of their class.

Drexel students earning the online BS in psychology explore how the human brain functions and how environmental and social variables influence cognitive development. One of the best online psychology degree programs in the country, the program's curriculum examines human development and why people act as they do. Located in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, Drexel's interdisciplinary program incorporates concepts in business, health sciences, neurosciences, education, and forensics. The fully online degree allows students to engage with peers and faculty through an interactive platform. Students examine psychological theory and strengthen their research abilities through courses in methods and statistics. Drexel admits transfer applicants with 24 college credits and first-time freshmen who graduated high school at least two years prior. All online students pay the same tuition rate, regardless of residency.
